title: She Of Little Faith

jokes.text: Right. Goggles on!
jokes.setup: Space air is flooding in - presumably a dangerous situation requiring protective gear
jokes.punchline: The solution to a space emergency is simple swimming goggles
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdist humor - treating a serious sci-fi situation with mundane logic. Economy is excellent, visual is implied but clear.

jokes.text: That's just a dog in a spacesuit! From the looks of it, a male dog.
jokes.setup: Monster from space threatens the ship
jokes.punchline: It's just a dog, and the casual anatomical observation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - not only deflates the tension but adds an unnecessary anatomical detail that makes it funnier. Visual comedy at its best.

jokes.text: Loser.
jokes.setup: Colonel Chet Manners introduces himself as 'five-time space shuttle alternate'
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - cutting through adult pretense with brutal honesty. Economy is perfect - one word that deflates the entire commercial.

jokes.text: Hey, Lise, is Dad's credit card number 5784365343410709? You know it is.
jokes.setup: Bart asking for credit card information
jokes.punchline: Lisa knows it by heart, implying this happens regularly
jokes.explanation: Dark family dynamic humor - kids casually using dad's credit without permission, and it's routine enough that Lisa has memorized the number.

jokes.text: Science? He didn't say 'science.' He said 'pie pants.' Mmm. Pie pants.
jokes.setup: Lisa thinks Homer is interested in science
jokes.punchline: Homer's mishearing reveals his true priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - mishearing something intellectual as something food-related, plus the dreamy 'Mmm. Pie pants' is quintessential Homer. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: My eyebrows! My beautiful eyebrows!
jokes.punchline: Homer's vain concern about his eyebrows being singed
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vanity joke - in a disaster, he focuses on his appearance. The word 'beautiful' makes it funnier by showing how seriously he takes his eyebrows.

jokes.text: The word 'unblowupable' is thrown around a lot these days, but I think I can say with confidence...
jokes.setup: Ned confidently claims something is unblowupable
jokes.punchline: Immediate explosion proves him wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff structure. The made-up word 'unblowupable' is funny, and the timing of the explosion right after his confident claim is impeccable.

jokes.text: Greetings from Neddedy Space Center on Cape Flandaveral.
jokes.punchline: Names that incorporate his own name and Flanders
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Ned's personality - his need to make everything about himself in an overly cheerful way. 'Flandaveral' is particularly clever.

jokes.text: 'Dweeb,' 'wonk,' 'spaz.' It's all good.
jokes.setup: Rocket enthusiasts ask Homer to stop calling them nerds
jokes.punchline: Homer offers alternative insults instead of stopping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely missing the point of their request. The casual 'It's all good' shows how oblivious he is to their feelings.

jokes.text: He's leaving her with five babies. She already ate three. That's sensible.
jokes.setup: Discussion about hamster saying goodbye to his wife
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of cannibalistic infanticide as practical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor - the casual 'That's sensible' delivery makes it even more disturbing and funny. Classic Simpsons subversive edge.

jokes.text: Son, we are about to break the surly bonds of gravity and punch the face of God!
jokes.punchline: Mixing poetic language with violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Fantastic parody of inspirational flight rhetoric with Homer's characteristic mix of grandeur and aggression. 'Punch the face of God' is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Five, four, three, two, one, countdown!
jokes.punchline: Homer adds redundant 'countdown' at the end
jokes.explanation: Small Homer moment showing his need to add unnecessary flourishes

jokes.text: Wow! That's it. I'm off the hooch. Hey, wine!
jokes.setup: Barney declares he's giving up alcohol after seeing the rocket
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by alcohol
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney character moment - the instant contradiction shows his complete lack of willpower. Economy is perfect with just two short phrases.

jokes.text: This is the worst thing you've ever done! You say that so much, it's lost all meaning.
jokes.setup: Marge declares this Homer's worst deed ever
jokes.punchline: Homer points out she says this constantly
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor that acknowledges a running pattern in the show while staying true to character dynamics. Homer's self-awareness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: All right! We'll help ourselves, yet again.
jokes.punchline: God's exasperated response about always having to help
jokes.explanation: God as overworked employee adds nice irreverent touch

jokes.text: Let's just write to David Bowie again. No, he's done enough for this church.
jokes.punchline: Implies David Bowie has already helped their church multiple times
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully random celebrity reference that creates a whole backstory in just two lines. The idea of David Bowie being a regular church donor is absurd.

jokes.text: That's just a skip-rope rhyme.
jokes.setup: Lovejoy says Burns has a reputation for being evil
jokes.punchline: Burns dismisses his evil reputation as playground gossip
jokes.explanation: Great Burns line - his casual dismissal of being called evil and reducing it to children's games shows his detachment from normal morality.

jokes.text: You'll get yours.
jokes.setup: Burns appears to be working with the church
jokes.punchline: Quiet threat showing his true nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - the quiet menace delivered casually. Shows he's already plotting even as he pretends to help.

jokes.text: Conflicted. Too bad! You've already signed the deal! Actually, he hasn't.
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's aggressive dismissal, Burns' correction
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's pushiness and Burns' attention to detail

jokes.text: Well, we highly value your input, until you sign the deal.
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that his opinion will become worthless after signing
jokes.explanation: Sharp business satire - the honest admission of how negotiation really works

jokes.text: The old church was skewing pious. We prefer a faith-based emporium, teeming with impulse-buy items.
jokes.punchline: Corporate speak that reduces faith to retail
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate culture invading religion. 'Skewing pious' as a problem perfectly captures the absurdity.

jokes.text: Why does Jesus have a lasso? Because he's all man.
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's macho interpretation of Christ
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious satire - the masculine rebranding of Jesus as a cowboy with the confident 'all man' justification is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Get your money changed, right here in the temple! That could not be more blasphemous.
jokes.punchline: Lisa recognizing the biblical parallel
jokes.explanation: Smart biblical reference to Jesus throwing out the money changers. Lisa's intellectual reaction provides the commentary for less-informed viewers.

jokes.text: Lisa, don't sulk. You're on the JumboTron. Adorable.
jokes.punchline: Her anger being broadcast and found cute
jokes.explanation: Technology in church undermining sincere emotion is decent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Trust me. He'll say it, or I'll bust him down to Thursday night vespers.
jokes.setup: Reverend hesitating to do commercial endorsement
jokes.punchline: Threatening him with the worst church assignment
jokes.explanation: Good insight into church hierarchy - Thursday night vespers as punishment duty is a believable detail that adds authenticity to the satire.

jokes.text: Quiet, Lisa. Everyone in the store is looking at you.
jokes.punchline: Marge accidentally calling church a store
jokes.explanation: Marge's Freudian slip reveals how much the church has changed. Her unconscious honesty about what it's become is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Like the Whore of Babylon? That is a false analogy. No, it's not. It's apt. Apt!
jokes.punchline: Her insistence on being technically correct
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - academic precision even when making inflammatory comparisons

jokes.text: Like the Whore of Babylon? That is a false analogy. No, it's not. It's apt. Apt!
jokes.setup: Lisa comparing commercialized church to biblical corruption
jokes.punchline: Her insistence on the aptness of her biblical reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - her intellectual outrage and insistence on the accuracy of her biblical analogy. The repeated 'Apt!' shows her passion.

jokes.text: He put ice in the urinals.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre luxury that no one asked for
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd detail that perfectly captures both Burns' weirdness and Homer's appreciation for bizarre luxuries.

jokes.text: Do you want your hand stamped so you can come back in? No! I'm leaving this church forever!
jokes.punchline: Mundane retail procedure offered
jokes.explanation: Good undermining of dramatic exit with retail bureaucracy

jokes.text: Oh, no! I don't know how to feel. You should be very upset. Got it. How is that? A little much.
jokes.setup: Homer needs guidance on emotional reaction
jokes.punchline: Marge directing his performance like a director
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment about performed emotions. Homer's complete emotional incompetence and Marge's patient direction is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Why do you have to be so different? Always making a big deal out of everything?
jokes.punchline: Marge reducing principled stand to teenage drama
jokes.explanation: Good mother-daughter dynamic but fairly standard

jokes.text: Honey, I'm worried about your soul. I want at least one person from this family to go to heaven.
jokes.punchline: Casual assumption rest of family is damned
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family's collective damnation delivered as caring parenting

jokes.text: Her? She's just kidding, Mr. Lord.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggests God might be female
jokes.punchline: Marge's panicked damage control to God
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - her nervousness about offending God and the awkward 'Mr. Lord' address shows her sincere but confused faith.

jokes.text: How about one of those religions where you eat a human heart? No. How about Methodist? No!
jokes.punchline: Equating cannibalistic rituals with Methodism
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke - treating extreme ritual cannibalism and mainstream Methodism as equivalent options. Lisa's equal rejection is the cherry on top.

jokes.text: How about Judaism? When you turn 13, cha-ching!
jokes.punchline: Reducing bar mitzvah to a money-making opportunity
jokes.explanation: Very Bart-like to focus on the financial benefits of religious ceremony. The 'cha-ching' sound effect makes it more vivid.

jokes.text: Yeah. If I didn't have inner peace, I'd completely go psycho on all you guys all the time.
jokes.punchline: Buddhism as anger management for violent tendencies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carl's underlying aggression held in check

jokes.text: What about the Dalai Lama? Who? You know, the 14th reincarnation of the Buddha Avalokiteshvara. Who's Buddha?
jokes.punchline: Supposed Buddhists don't know basic Buddhism
jokes.explanation: Good satire of superficial spirituality. Lenny and Carl being Buddhists who don't know Buddha reveals their shallow understanding.

jokes.text: It's a good thing Buddhism teaches freedom from desire, 'cause I've got the desire to kick your ass.
jokes.punchline: Buddhist threatening violence while citing Buddhism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - using Buddhist philosophy to justify un-Buddhist behavior. The self-awareness makes it funnier than simple hypocrisy.

jokes.text: I dream about meatball sandwiches, all-you-can-eat for two bucks.
jokes.setup: Richard Gere talking about Tibet dreams
jokes.punchline: Carl's much more mundane food dreams
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but fairly predictable blue-collar contrast

jokes.text: Hey, Richard, in An Officer and a Gentleman, did you really do all those sit-ups? I wish. I did one, and they just showed it 1,000 times.
jokes.punchline: Behind-the-scenes movie magic reality
jokes.explanation: Decent Hollywood satire about movie magic, though not particularly character-specific or memorable compared to other jokes in the episode.

jokes.text: Hey, Richard, in An Officer and a Gentleman, did you really do all those sit-ups? I wish. I did one, and they just showed it 1,000 times.
jokes.punchline: Honest admission about film editing tricks
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor about movie magic but nothing special

jokes.text: My Satan-sense is tingling.
jokes.punchline: Spider-sense parody for detecting evil
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned line - combining his religious paranoia with pop culture reference. The economy is perfect and it's highly quotable.

jokes.text: My Satan-sense is tingling. Into the root cellar, boys! When can we come out? Maybe never. Yay! Yay!
jokes.punchline: Kids' enthusiasm for indefinite hiding
jokes.explanation: Good Flanders family dynamic - kids' cheerful acceptance of religious paranoia

jokes.text: Hold that thought. Bacon up that sausage, boy.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to have serious conversation
jokes.punchline: Homer's food priorities interrupt everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - 'bacon up that sausage' is delightfully excessive and his timing shows complete obliviousness to emotional moments.

jokes.text: But, Dad, my heart hurts.
jokes.setup: Homer forcing Bart to prepare meat despite family crisis
jokes.punchline: Bart's genuine emotional pain amid the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's obliviousness and Bart's rare moment of emotional vulnerability. Creates sympathy while maintaining absurdity.

jokes.text: You know, Lisa, around here, Buddhists don't get any desserts in their lunches. A Buddhist wouldn't want any.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to pressure Lisa about Buddhism
jokes.punchline: Lisa's logical counter to the threat
jokes.explanation: Good dynamic showing Marge's clumsy manipulation tactics and Lisa's intelligence in deflecting them with Buddhist philosophy.

jokes.text: His name's Gunnar, and he's dating my mom. Sometimes, he buys us beer. I thought Kearney was dating your mom. Hey, she came on to me!
jokes.setup: Discussion about someone with long hair and crazy ideas
jokes.punchline: Reveals dysfunctional family situations among bullies
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about dysfunctional families. The escalation from Gunnar to Kearney to defensive protest creates good comedic rhythm.

jokes.text: Sometimes, he buys us beer. I thought Kearney was dating your mom. Hey, she came on to me!
jokes.punchline: Multiple bullies dating same mom, Kearney's defensive protest
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of dysfunctional family situation

jokes.text: You're gonna get a bath tonight!
jokes.punchline: Threatening a bath as if it's violence
jokes.explanation: Mild Homer absurdity - treating hygiene as punishment. Works for character but not particularly memorable or clever.

jokes.text: When hell freezes over, suckers!
jokes.setup: Church asking when they'll see the revenue
jokes.punchline: Burns reveals his true plan
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns reveal of his scheme. The 'suckers!' adds insult to injury and is very much in character for his gleeful evil.

jokes.text: And Santa doesn't leave presents under the Bodhi Tree.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Christmas and Lisa's Buddhism
jokes.punchline: Practical consequence of religious choice
jokes.explanation: Good practical joke about religious consequences. The specificity of the Bodhi Tree makes it feel more authentic than generic religious humor.

jokes.text: Time to begin Operation X-Mas Remind of How Good Is.
jokes.setup: Homer planning to win Lisa back to Christmas
jokes.punchline: Poorly constructed operation name
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer grammar failure. The awkward phrasing perfectly captures his limited vocabulary while trying to sound official.

jokes.text: I think I ate a dog food lid.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of eating non-food item
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - the casual delivery of something alarming. The specificity of 'dog food lid' makes it more vivid and believable.

jokes.text: I think I ate a dog food lid.
jokes.punchline: Eating so frantically she consumes non-food
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of cookie binge to absurd extreme

jokes.text: I just know his name is Clip-Clop and he loves sugar.
jokes.setup: Marge claiming ignorance about what Santa left
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific knowledge of the supposed gift
jokes.explanation: Great contradiction - claiming not to know while providing specific details. 'Clip-Clop' is a perfectly generic pony name that makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I was only about to achieve enlightenment. But who'd want that?
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic dismissal of the ultimate Buddhist goal
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan sarcasm about the highest goal in Buddhism. The casual dismissal of enlightenment is perfectly ironic and quotable.

jokes.text: I did it! I found our dog! Now our Christmas is complete! We were looking for Lisa! I thought we were caroling.
jokes.punchline: Complete confusion about the mission
jokes.explanation: Good escalating confusion showing how scattered the Simpson family can be. Each character having a different understanding is classic family dysfunction.

jokes.text: I did it! I found our dog! Now our Christmas is complete! We were looking for Lisa!
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused priorities
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer confusion - mixing up family priorities

jokes.text: So, you're just going to pay lip service to our church? Uh-huh. That's all I ever asked.
jokes.punchline: His satisfaction with minimal participation
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of religious institutions being satisfied with superficial compliance. Lovejoy's honesty about his low expectations is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Here, Clip-Clop! Here, pony-pony!
jokes.setup: Earlier mention of the pony named Clip-Clop
jokes.punchline: Homer still looking for the non-existent pony
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Homer's persistence and gullibility. The 'pony-pony' call is endearingly childish for a grown man.

jokes.text: I'm serious. Make with the pony. Here, Clip-Clop! Here, pony-pony!
jokes.punchline: Demanding materialistic reward after spiritual journey
jokes.explanation: Decent ending showing Lisa hasn't completely transcended material desires

tmdb_overview: After Homer and Bart's model rocket damages the church, Mr. Burns makes a deal to commercialize the church in return for paying for the damages. Lisa becomes disgusted at what the church has become, so she decides to find a new religion suitable for her. She eventually converts to Buddhism, causing Marge to fear for Lisa's soul.
